<item>
      <title>Blood on the Tungabhadra: When Total War Came to Vijayanagara</title>
      <description>Sultan Muhammad Shah Bahmani launches total war against Vijayanagara in 1366, inflicting catastrophic defeats on Bukka Raya — including a massacre at the Tungabhadra battlefield where even pregnant women and infants were slaughtered, and a devastating night attack that caught the king drinking with dancing-women — ultimately claiming 500,000 Hindu lives before peace is bought. Years later, the young Sultan Mujahid penetrates to the very heart of the capital and destroys a sacred jeweled temple, only to be assassinated by his own uncle Daud Khan, whose treachery gives Bukka the opening to reclaim the disputed Doab territories. Based on "A Forgotten Empire (Vijayanagara)" by Robert Sewell (1900).</description>
      <enclosure url="https://drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id=1Rbmk3tdRDrUPSiWczM_ITL6IngeVF8al" type="audio/mpeg" length="47189724" />
